title: IDF Strikes 120 Hezbollah Targets in Southern Lebanon in Single Day Amid Ceasefire
summary: The Israeli military struck approximately 120 Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon within 24 hours, dismantling 70 military structures and 50 infrastructure sites across multiple areas despite the extended ceasefire.
contents:
## Massive Israeli Strike Campaign in Southern Lebanon

### Operational Scope
The Israel Defense Forces reported striking approximately 120 Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon within a single day. The strikes dismantled approximately 70 military structures and approximately 50 Hezbollah infrastructure sites across several areas, according to the IDF statement.

### Target Categories
Israeli operations targeted weapons storage facilities, command posts, tunnel entrances, and positions the military identified as launch sites for rockets and drones. The comprehensive strike pattern covered multiple sectors of southern Lebanon simultaneously.

### Ceasefire Contradiction
The intensity of operations contradicted the US-brokered ceasefire framework. The IDF maintained that operations fell within its reserved right to respond to "planned, imminent or ongoing attacks" — a provision Israel interprets broadly to justify continued offensive action.

### Hezbollah Counter-Operations
Hezbollah continued drone attacks against IDF positions in southern Lebanon throughout the period. The group claimed responsibility for strikes using first-person-view (FPV) drones, some guided via fiber-optic cables making them immune to electronic jamming. Multiple Hezbollah drones targeted Israeli troops, with some intercepted and others causing casualties.

### Strategic Assessment
Crisis Group analysts warned that continued fighting at this intensity threatens to "scupper any hope of making the ceasefire stick." The monitoring mechanism established by the November 2024 agreement requires strengthening to constrain both parties.

**Sources:** TASS/IDF statement, Crisis Group analysis, Yeni Safak
